title: Bootleg
wikitext:
[]: Bootleg             Quote Post by Number 44 » Sat May 01, 2010 9:05 pm I finally got around to ordering a copy of Death and Rebirth. Found a new copy dirt cheap. I was leery, so I dug up reviews on the site I was going to order it from. Nothing but positive reviews. So I ordered it. This is what arrived.... [URL=http://img85.imageshack.us/i/coverdy.jpg/] [/URL] [URL=http://img440.imageshack.us/i/backsz.jpg/] [/URL] [URL=http://img25.imageshack.us/i/dvdma.jpg/] [/URL] Long question short, did I end up with a bootleg? Feel free to turn this into a general bootleg topic, after I get an answer.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Sat May 01, 2010 9:27 pm No copyright information, it's probably a bootleg. Also, when I scan that barcode, I get back some HK Heavy Metal L-Gaim and a Zone of the Enders DVD.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sat May 01, 2010 9:31 pm Yes. Bootleg.

[]:             Quote Post by drinian » Sat May 01, 2010 9:41 pm Both films on the same disc? Definitely a bootleg. Also, the disc says "Disc 4," which I guess implies that you can get a bootleg of the rest of the series on discs 1, 2, and 3. My best advice is: 1) Ebay, Half.com, and Amazon.com Marketplace sellers are rife with anime piracy, because the rights holders don't crack down like with American movies. I would only deal with individual sellers (not businesses) on Ebay who are clearly selling stuff from their collection and have posted pictures of the actual box. 2) The only anime-specific Web stores I'm aware of that don't stock pirated goods are Right Stuf and Robert's Anime Corner Store ; I have no experience ordering from the latter, but Right Stuf usually has their act together. 3) Between Amazon, Right Stuf, and Deep Discount, you should be able to find the cheapest possible price for new, legitimate discs. Otherwise, I use http://www.dvdaf.com to search other stores. They have the most comprehensive database of DVDs out there. 4) If it's too good to be true -- it is. Especially beware of places selling shows that haven't been licensed in the US, or selling 26 episodes on 3 discs, with Chinese subtitles, etc. 5) Reviews aren't that helpful, since a lot of people still have no qualms about buying pirated, or just don't know somehow. I have my share of fansubs, but I don't like financially rewarding piracy. That reminds me, was the Renewal version of EoE ever released in Region 1?

[]: Re: Bootleg             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sun May 02, 2010 1:03 am Number 44 wrote: [URL=http://img25.imageshack.us/i/dvdma.jpg/] [/URL]

[]:             Quote Post by kirri » Sun May 02, 2010 3:15 pm I ended up with that exact same bootleg. Seemed to play both the movies perfectly fine though. Did you happen to order it at ToyWuala.com too?

[]:             Quote Post by Number 44 » Sun May 02, 2010 10:41 pm Yes, actually.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon May 03, 2010 1:48 am I bought one bootleg Eva item and I am forever ashamed. Time for my weekly flogging.

[]:             Quote Post by drinian » Mon May 03, 2010 3:51 am From the website you two got your pirated copy from (did you order a Pure Silver NFL Peyton Manning Commemorative Coin too?): Region : All Region, Free Code

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Mon May 03, 2010 1:38 pm kirri wrote: I ended up with that exact same bootleg. Seemed to play both the movies perfectly fine though. Did you happen to order it at ToyWuala.com too?

[]:             Quote Post by SenorSquiid » Mon May 03, 2010 8:43 pm I got Death and Rebirth on a umpteenth generation fansubbed VHS years ago before the manga release, and I ended up pissed because it was labeled End of Evangelion, but wasn't, and I ended up STILL having to wait for the Manga release. That said, I miss those days and get nostalgic sometimes for the glowy fuzzy images and faraway sound and Chinese subtitles under English. And the credits attributed it to Cartman's Mom productions and has some fairly funny gag credits.

[]:             Quote Post by duexmachina » Tue May 04, 2010 12:55 pm dont feel bad,a couple of years ago when i first got into eva i bought this eva "boxset" which had the whole series and both movies for the around $60.(i think disc 4 was the same one you posted.) i didn't know i was being ripped off at the time but im happy with my purchase.

[]:             Quote Post by Sharaz Destler » Tue May 04, 2010 3:59 pm I got mine at the flea market last year. By all rights, I should feel ashamed, but I don't. It helps that the originals are out of print.

[]:             Quote Post by skikes » Tue May 04, 2010 9:26 pm Surely they had pictures of the item before you ordered it? How could you not have known... I suspect that you suspected that this was bootleg all along. GET YOURSELF SOME BITTORRENTS! The price will be even better.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Wed May 05, 2010 8:26 am skikes wrote: Surely they had pictures of the item before you ordered it? How could you not have known...

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Wed May 05, 2010 8:30 am My copies of EoE and D&R are also bootlegs, albeit a good bootleg, much better than whatever you got (although, it does cut off the "Komichi Waru" line halfway in). I've been meaning to buy myself new copies, so maybe when I have a bit more money.

[]:             Quote Post by Apjak » Wed May 05, 2010 9:10 am My First copies of D&R and EoE were Chinese. The upside is that I actually got to see Rebirth because of it, instead of just a copypasta of the First 45 min of EOE. No thanks to Manga™.

[]:             Quote Post by master_lloyd » Thu May 06, 2010 10:34 am My copy of Death and Rebirth is a bootleg too, maybe we should start up a support group? On the back of the case it promises the film will "take you beyond the death of the 27th Evangelion." A promise it sadly could not fulfill...

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Thu May 06, 2010 6:32 pm Look on the bright side. The quality might actually turn out to be better than the Manga release.

[]:             Quote Post by GasmaskAvenger » Thu May 06, 2010 7:19 pm reminds me of when I ordered "Evangelion: The Birthday of Rei Ayanami" off of amazon and received a bootleg copy instead however, at least it provided me with a backcover, in which the real copy doesn't have at all as for my copy of EoE...I actually own a copy of the US Manga DVD, but I didn't know that this (and Death & Rebirth) are being bootlegged
